plan.topic: Restructure codex-nexus repo to stop tracking project-scope install artifacts in .codex/.agents

issues.title: Separate publishable plugin sources from project-scope install artifacts so repo-root .codex/.agents no longer need to be tracked while sync, validate, and install guarantees remain intact.
issues.decision: Selected approach: treat plugins/codex-nexus/** as the only tracked publishable plugin source, stop using repo-root .codex/.agents as tracked validation fixtures, narrow sync-from-nexus-core.mjs to sync publishable plugin assets only, and move install-behavior verification to tests that create temporary installs. Update validation and layout tests so they verify publishable assets plus installer behavior instead of requiring tracked repo-root .codex/.agents. Rejected alternative A: keep the current mixed model and only untrack .codex/config.toml and .codex/hooks.json; dismissed because .agents/.codex would remain semantically ambiguous and repo self-install artifacts would still leak into source control. Rejected alternative B: keep tracked fixtures but move them to test/fixtures/project-install; dismissed because stable fixture snapshots would still duplicate installer behavior already covered by installer tests and would add more sync surface to maintain.

plan.research_summary: Inspected scripts/sync-from-nexus-core.mjs, scripts/validate-plugin.mjs, test/plugin-layout.test.js, test/installer.test.js, README, package manifest, and current git state. Findings: (1) plugins/codex-nexus/** is the publishable source of truth; package.files excludes .codex and .agents. (2) Repo-root .codex/.agents are currently treated as tracked generated fixtures: sync-from-nexus-core.mjs writes .codex/agents, .codex/lead.instructions.md, .codex/config.toml, and .agents/skills into the repo root. (3) validate-plugin.mjs and plugin-layout.test.js assert repo-root .codex/.agents exist, while installer.test already validates true project-scope and user-scope installs in temporary directories. (4) .codex/config.toml, .codex/hooks.json, and .codex/packages are local install outputs and create git noise; current branch already has local modifications there. (5) No prior Nexus history exists for this topic.

tasks.title: Refactor repo to stop tracking project-scope install artifacts under .codex/.agents
tasks.context: Repo currently mixes publishable plugin sources (plugins/codex-nexus/**) with tracked project-scope install outputs under .codex and .agents. sync-from-nexus-core.mjs writes repo-root .codex/.agents, validate-plugin.mjs and plugin-layout.test.js require them, and local installs create git noise. installer.test already covers real project/user installs in temp directories.
tasks.acceptance: 1. Repo no longer requires tracked repo-root .codex/.agents artifacts for validate/check/test. 2. scripts/sync-from-nexus-core.mjs syncs publishable plugin assets without rewriting repo-root project-install outputs. 3. .gitignore ignores repo-root .codex/ and .agents/ as local install artifacts. 4. Previously tracked repo-root .codex/.agents files are removed from git index but remain ignored locally. 5. bun run validate and bun run check both pass after the refactor.
tasks.approach: Make plugins/codex-nexus/** the tracked source of truth. Update sync-from-nexus-core.mjs to stop writing repo-root .codex/.agents. Narrow validate-plugin.mjs and plugin-layout.test.js to publishable/plugin-source checks, relying on installer.test for install-path behavior. Update .gitignore and repo docs accordingly, then untrack repo-root .codex/.agents artifacts from git while leaving local files on disk.
tasks.risk: Validation/test assumptions may still implicitly depend on repo-root .codex/.agents, causing breakage until all references are removed.

plan.topic: Fix recurring subagent nx MCP startup failure in codex-nexus installs

issues.title: Identify the precise root cause and authoritative fix shape for subagent nx MCP configuration

analysis.summary: Root cause is a configuration mismatch between the main Codex session and spawned subagents. The installer writes `.codex/config.toml` so the main session launches the `nx` MCP server through the current runtime plus an absolute `@moreih29/nexus-core/dist/mcp/server.js` path, explicitly avoiding PATH-dependent commands. But every generated non-lead subagent TOML overrides `[mcp_servers.nx]` with `command = "nexus-mcp"`, which requires a bare executable on PATH. In this repo, `command -v nexus-mcp` is empty, matching the observed `MCP startup failed: No such file or directory (os error 2)`. Official Codex docs say MCP servers are configured under `config.toml`, and custom agent files are configuration layers; optional fields like `mcp_servers` inherit from the parent when omitted. Public Codex issue reports (#2945, #4754) show the same class of failure when the configured MCP command cannot be launched.

issues.decision: Selected approach: treat the failure as a bad subagent MCP launcher configuration, not as an nx MCP server defect. The authoritative fix should make subagent agent files launch `nx` with a resolvable command instead of bare `nexus-mcp`, while preserving the existing `disabled_tools` restrictions for each role. Rationale: official Codex docs define MCP launch through `mcp_servers.<id>.command/args` in config, and the current installer already uses a concrete runtime + absolute server path for the main session; the bug appears only because spawned subagents override that with an unresolved command. Rejected alternatives: (1) ask users to install `nexus-mcp` globally or patch PATH — rejected because it contradicts the package’s documented promise to avoid PATH dependence and would remain fragile across environments; (2) leave source files unchanged and only document the limitation — rejected because the failure is deterministic and breaks a core workflow; (3) remove subagent `mcp_servers.nx` sections entirely and rely on inheritance — rejected because it would also remove current per-role `disabled_tools` restrictions unless we rely on subtler merge behavior than needed.

issues.title: Implement the code change so installed subagents no longer depend on a missing bare `nexus-mcp` executable

analysis.summary: The safest repair is two-stage normalization. First, normalize tracked plugin agent specs after `sync:core` so they no longer ship a bare `nexus-mcp` command; instead they should use the same pinned package-based launcher shape as the plugin `.mcp.json` (`command = "npx"` plus `-y -p @moreih29/nexus-core@<version> nexus-mcp`). Second, during `codex-nexus install`, rewrite the copied agent TOMLs in both the installed plugin copy and `.codex/agents` to the already-proven local-runtime shape (`command = <process.execPath>`, `args = [<absolute dist/mcp/server.js>]`). This preserves disabled tool lists, keeps publishable source self-consistent, and preserves the installer’s stronger PATH-independent guarantee for real user installs.

issues.decision: Selected implementation: introduce shared helper logic that rewrites the `[mcp_servers.nx]` block in generated agent TOMLs while preserving each file’s `disabled_tools`. Use that helper in two places: (a) `sync-from-nexus-core.mjs` to rewrite publishable plugin agent specs to a pinned `npx -y -p @moreih29/nexus-core@<version> nexus-mcp` launcher, and (b) `codex-nexus.mjs` install flow to rewrite copied agent specs to the stronger runtime-specific launcher (`process.execPath` + absolute installed `dist/mcp/server.js` path). Also teach install health checks to catch regressions. Rationale: this matches official Codex MCP configuration semantics, aligns source/plugin/install layers, and avoids reliance on a globally installed executable. Rejected alternatives: (1) patch only `.codex/config.toml` — rejected because subagent agent files currently override the server and would still win; (2) patch only source plugin files to `npx` and stop there — rejected because it still leaves installed subagents PATH-dependent when the installer already promises a stronger invariant; (3) patch only installed `.codex/agents` and ignore plugin copies/source — rejected because it leaves the package’s publishable artifacts internally inconsistent and easier to regress on the next sync.

issues.title: Add regression coverage so future syncs or releases do not reintroduce PATH-dependent subagent MCP launch behavior

analysis.summary: Current tests verify the main `.codex/config.toml` uses a resolved server path, but they never assert anything about installed subagent TOMLs. That gap allowed the regression. Coverage should assert both layers: source plugin agent specs should not contain `command = "nexus-mcp"`, and installed `.codex/agents/*.toml` should inherit the installer’s runtime-specific command plus absolute server path. A lightweight doctor/validation check can fail fast on broken installs before the user reaches a subagent flow.

issues.decision: Selected regression strategy: expand automated tests and lightweight validation to cover subagent MCP launch configuration explicitly. Add assertions that tracked plugin agent specs do not use a bare `nexus-mcp` command, and that installed `.codex/agents` use the installer runtime plus absolute `dist/mcp/server.js` path. Add a doctor/validation signal for malformed installed agent MCP blocks. Rationale: the bug only manifested in spawned subagents, so config-level coverage on both source and installed artifacts is the right permanent guardrail. Rejected alternatives: (1) rely only on manual reproduction with spawned agents — rejected because it is slow, brittle, and hard to automate in CI; (2) add only README guidance — rejected because documentation cannot prevent silent regression in generated config; (3) add only one source-side test — rejected because the real failure happened after installation, so both source and installed outputs must be checked.

plan.research_summary: Local repo evidence: the installer intentionally writes .codex/config.toml with an absolute runtime command plus the installed @moreih29/nexus-core dist/mcp/server.js path, explicitly avoiding PATH-dependent npx/nexus-mcp launch. However every generated non-lead subagent TOML under plugins/codex-nexus/agents hardcodes [mcp_servers.nx] command = "nexus-mcp" plus disabled_tools. In this repo, `command -v nexus-mcp` returns nothing, while node_modules/.bin/nexus-mcp and node_modules/@moreih29/nexus-core/dist/mcp/server.js do exist. README also promises that nx MCP is wired through the installer runtime and absolute server path rather than relying on PATH. Official Codex docs say custom agent files are configuration layers and optional fields such as mcp_servers inherit from the parent session when omitted; Codex MCP docs define command/args/cwd on [mcp_servers.<id>] and note project/user config.toml is the canonical MCP config surface. OpenAI Codex docs: https://developers.openai.com/codex/subagents, https://developers.openai.com/codex/mcp, https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-reference. Similar public cases exist in openai/codex issue tracker where MCP startup fails with program not found / os error 2 when the configured command cannot be launched, e.g. issue #2945 and #4754.

tasks.title: Add reusable nx subagent MCP normalization and apply it during install
tasks.context: Subagent agent TOMLs currently hardcode `[mcp_servers.nx] command = "nexus-mcp"`, which fails with `No such file or directory (os error 2)` when Codex spawns child agents and the bare executable is not on PATH. The installer already knows the correct runtime command and absolute `@moreih29/nexus-core/dist/mcp/server.js` path.
tasks.acceptance: `codex-nexus install` rewrites installed subagent TOMLs so they no longer contain `command = "nexus-mcp"`; installed `.codex/agents/*.toml` use the runtime command and absolute installed server path; role-specific `disabled_tools` entries remain intact.
tasks.approach: Introduce helper logic to rewrite the `[mcp_servers.nx]` block in agent TOMLs while preserving each file’s `disabled_tools`. Use it during `codex-nexus install` so both the installed plugin copy and `.codex/agents` point to `process.execPath` plus the resolved `dist/mcp/server.js` path.
tasks.risk: If TOML rewriting is too broad it could accidentally disturb long instruction blocks or unrelated agent configuration.

tasks.title: Normalize tracked plugin agent specs and add install-time validation
tasks.context: The publishable plugin artifacts under `plugins/codex-nexus/agents` are the tracked source of truth for generated Nexus agent files. They currently embed a PATH-dependent nx launcher and can regress again on the next sync if the sync step does not normalize them.
tasks.acceptance: Tracked plugin agent TOMLs no longer contain bare `command = "nexus-mcp"`; sync output is normalized automatically; validation/doctor catches malformed installed agent nx MCP blocks.
tasks.approach: Teach the sync pipeline to rewrite tracked plugin agent TOMLs to a pinned `npx -y -p @moreih29/nexus-core@<version> nexus-mcp` launcher, and extend validation/doctor checks so broken installed agent MCP wiring is reported before users hit a subagent flow.
tasks.risk: If publishable source and installed output normalization diverge, the package may become internally inconsistent again.

tasks.title: Add regression tests for source and installed subagent nx MCP config
tasks.context: Current test coverage checks the main Codex config but not the child-agent TOMLs that actually fail when Nexus spawns subagents.
tasks.acceptance: Automated tests fail if tracked plugin agent TOMLs regress to `command = "nexus-mcp"`; automated tests fail if installed `.codex/agents` omit the runtime command or server path; the existing test suite passes after the fix.
tasks.approach: Extend plugin layout and installer tests to assert that source plugin agent specs do not use bare `nexus-mcp`, and that installed `.codex/agents` use the installer runtime command and absolute `dist/mcp/server.js` path.
tasks.risk: Assertions may be too brittle if they depend on formatting rather than the semantic config content.

plan.topic: Update codex-nexus for nexus-core v0.19.1 and deploy a release

issues.title: Choose the correct downstream change set for adopting nexus-core v0.19.1 without retaining conflicting MCP workaround behavior

analysis.summary: Upstream v0.19.1 removes the root cause that codex-nexus had worked around: generated Codex child agents no longer hardcode `command = "nexus-mcp"` and instead preserve the parent session's MCP launcher. Keeping our downstream child-agent rewrite logic would now fight the intended upstream inheritance model, create unnecessary divergence, and force source/install behavior that upstream explicitly says consumers can remove. However, our main-session install logic that writes `.codex/config.toml` with a runtime + absolute installed server path remains valuable and should stay, because it solves parent-session PATH dependence. Therefore the correct scope is: keep parent-session MCP installation behavior, drop child-agent-specific rewrite/normalization workarounds, and resync generated assets from upstream 0.19.1.

issues.decision: Selected approach: adopt nexus-core v0.19.1 as the new source of truth for Codex child-agent MCP behavior, keeping only codex-nexus's parent-session install wiring and removing downstream child-agent nx MCP rewrite logic. Rationale: upstream now intentionally preserves the parent launcher and explicitly says downstream Codex consumers can remove those workarounds; retaining them would unnecessarily override the inherited launcher model and increase maintenance burden. Rejected alternatives: (1) keep all local rewrite logic while also bumping upstream — rejected because it preserves divergence after the upstream fix; (2) remove all MCP wiring, including parent-session absolute-path install config — rejected because parent-session PATH-independence is still a codex-nexus responsibility and remains documented behavior; (3) postpone release until more upstream releases land — rejected because v0.19.1 directly fixes the issue we filed and the wrapper should align promptly.

issues.title: Implement the update: bump dependency, remove or simplify obsolete MCP rewrite logic, resync generated assets, and adjust validation/tests

analysis.summary: The implementation delta is clear from local code search: remove `scripts/lib/nx-agent-mcp.mjs`, stop calling its normalization helpers from install/sync/validate/tests, rerun `sync:core` after bumping `@moreih29/nexus-core` to 0.19.1 so `plugins/codex-nexus/agents/*`, skills, and lead instructions are regenerated, and update tests to assert the new upstream behavior (child-agent MCP stanza should not hardcode bare nexus-mcp and should preserve disabled_tools while allowing parent inheritance). Also update any validation assumptions and published-files list that only existed for the workaround helper.

issues.decision: Selected implementation: bump @moreih29/nexus-core to 0.19.1, run the official sync flow to regenerate publishable skills/agents/instructions, then delete the downstream child-agent MCP normalization helper and all call sites/tests that depended on it. Replace them with checks that match the upstream contract: generated child agents keep per-role disabled_tools under `[mcp_servers.nx]` but do not force a launcher, while installed parent config still points to the runtime plus absolute server path. Rejected alternatives: (1) hand-edit generated plugin agent files without rerunning sync — rejected because it fights the generated source-of-truth model; (2) keep the helper only for install-time doctor — rejected because it would keep dead code and misleading assertions; (3) skip validation changes and trust upstream blindly — rejected because the wrapper still owns packaging, install surfaces, and release safety.

issues.title: Prepare and execute the release/deploy path, including version bump, changelog/release notes, validation, tag push, publish monitoring, and post-publish checks

analysis.summary: Current published version is 0.3.9, so the next safe release is a patch bump to 0.3.10. This release should include the upstream alignment plus removal of obsolete workaround code and the new generated assets. Release execution should follow the repository checklist: full validation locally, version bump/changelog, commit, tag `v0.3.10`, push branch and tag, monitor `validate.yml` and `publish-npm.yml`, confirm npm shows 0.3.10, run a fresh install/doctor smoke check against the published package, and create/update the GitHub Release if the workflow does not do that automatically.

issues.decision: Selected release plan: publish codex-nexus 0.3.10 as a patch release. Reasoning: 0.3.9 is already the latest published version, and this change is a compatibility/bug-fix alignment with upstream v0.19.1 rather than a feature or breaking change. Execute the checklist-driven release path locally, then deploy through the existing trusted-publishing GitHub Actions workflow by pushing tag `v0.3.10`. Rejected alternatives: (1) reuse version 0.3.9 — rejected because npm already has it; (2) publish 0.4.0 — rejected because the scope is not a breaking feature release; (3) stop at code changes without deployment — rejected because the user explicitly asked to deploy.

plan.research_summary: Current published codex-nexus version is 0.3.9 on npm and GitHub. Local package.json is 0.3.9 and depends on @moreih29/nexus-core 0.19.0. Upstream nexus-core released v0.19.1 on 2026-04-22. Release notes explicitly say Codex child-agent specs no longer hardcode a bare nexus-mcp launcher and downstream Codex consumers can remove workarounds that rewrote child-agent mcp_servers.nx.command. PR #62 / issue #59 confirm the intended behavior: preserve parent nx MCP launcher and keep only disabled_tools in child-agent MCP blocks by default. Local codex-nexus still carries a downstream workaround: scripts/lib/nx-agent-mcp.mjs plus installer/sync/validation/tests that normalize child-agent nx MCP config. Release checklist in .nexus/context/releasing.md requires updating changelog, running bun install, bun run sync:core, bun run validate, bun test, node scripts/codex-nexus.mjs --help, doctor --scope project, npm pack --dry-run, and completing post-publish checks. Local npm CLI is not authenticated, but this repo has a trusted-publishing GitHub Actions workflow that publishes on tag push. GitHub release notes say downstream wrappers should reinstall/resync assets from @moreih29/nexus-core@0.19.1.

tasks.title: Update dependency and regenerate Nexus assets from upstream v0.19.1
tasks.context: Published codex-nexus 0.3.9 still depends on @moreih29/nexus-core 0.19.0. Upstream v0.19.1 fixes the Codex child-agent MCP launcher inheritance issue and includes related generated instruction updates.
tasks.acceptance: package.json and lockfile reference @moreih29/nexus-core 0.19.1; generated publishable assets are resynced from upstream; repo sources are internally consistent after sync.
tasks.approach: Bump @moreih29/nexus-core to 0.19.1, refresh the lockfile, and run the official sync flow so publishable plugin assets and lead/skill text are regenerated from upstream.
tasks.risk: Generated assets may overwrite local customizations if any were intentionally diverged from upstream.

tasks.title: Remove obsolete child-agent nx MCP workaround code and align validation/tests
tasks.context: codex-nexus currently contains downstream helper and rewrite logic that normalized child-agent nx MCP blocks to work around an upstream issue now fixed in nexus-core 0.19.1.
tasks.acceptance: No downstream code rewrites child-agent nx MCP launcher blocks solely to avoid bare nexus-mcp; validation and tests assert the new upstream contract; the repository no longer publishes dead workaround helper code.
tasks.approach: Delete the no-longer-needed normalization helper, remove install/sync/validation logic that rewrites child-agent launchers, and update tests to assert the new upstream child-agent inheritance behavior while keeping parent-session install checks.
tasks.risk: If the new upstream contract is misunderstood, tests may accidentally validate the wrong inheritance behavior.

tasks.title: Prepare release metadata and validate the 0.3.10 package
tasks.context: The next deployable version must be greater than the currently published npm version 0.3.9 and must satisfy the repo release checklist before tagging/publishing.
tasks.acceptance: package.json and related release metadata are 0.3.10; CHANGELOG documents the release; bun install/sync/validate/test, CLI help, doctor, and npm pack --dry-run all pass.
tasks.approach: Bump package version to 0.3.10, update CHANGELOG with user-visible changes, run the full checklist commands plus smoke checks needed for confidence, and ensure package contents are correct.
tasks.risk: Release metadata or package contents could drift from the actual code changes if updated too early or too late.

tasks.title: Deploy 0.3.10 via tag-triggered publish workflow and complete post-publish checks
tasks.context: This repository uses GitHub Actions trusted publishing on tag push; local npm auth is not available, so deployment must complete through the remote workflow and follow-up verification.
tasks.acceptance: Release commit and tag v0.3.10 are pushed; publish workflow succeeds; npm shows codex-nexus 0.3.10; fresh install/help/doctor smoke checks pass against the published package; a GitHub Release exists for v0.3.10.
tasks.approach: Commit the release changes, create/push tag v0.3.10, monitor validate/publish workflows, verify npm shows 0.3.10, run a fresh published-package smoke test, and create/publish the GitHub Release if needed.
tasks.risk: Remote CI or trusted publishing configuration may fail after tagging, which would block full deployment despite local validation.
